reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Verified accurate. Blob sidecar validation enforces max_blobs_per_block check at fork_choice_store/src/store.rs:1838-1846, but data column sidecar validation (L2033-2219) omits this check. The verify_data_column_sidecar helper (eip_7594/src/lib.rs:147-172) only validates structural constraints (index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, non-empty commitments, equal array lengths) but does NOT check kzg_commitments.len() <= max_blobs_per_block(epoch). Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a data column sidecar with kzg_commitments.len() up to 4096 (SSZ limit) while the protocol limit is only 21. KZG proof verification (verify_kzg_proofs at L2195) executes during gossip validation BEFORE block processing, causing CPU exhaustion proportional to commitment count. The block-level check (transition_functions/src/fulu/block_processing.rs:186-194) happens after gossip validation and does not prevent the DoS. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-009 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (L227-242) requires 'forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block'. The code violates this invariant for data column sidecars. Severity: Impact is resource exhaustion on >5% of network (all Grandine nodes) via single malicious P2P packet, matching High severity criteria per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. Attack path: attacker sends data_column_sidecar via P2P gossip with 4096 commitments (vs. limit of 21), causing excessive KZG verification during gossip validation (L2195), resulting in CPU exhaustion before the block-level check can reject it.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch. Assertion: forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block (from 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json)
